exact.cm_orientation: The transported A_t^(1) is a positive Mellin moment family and has nonnegative Hankel minors (ordinary log-convex orientation).
exact.complete_monotonicity_failure: d_u phi_1(0)>0, so g'(y)>0 for sufficiently small y>0; g is not completely monotone
exact.live_replacement: A signed reciprocal-Gamma transport theorem or the direct stable-gap curvature certificate is still required.
exact.source_scope: The source theorem treats positive Mellin/Hankel total positivity and explicitly separates reciprocal sign-regularity.

primary_source.author: Domingos S. P. Salazar
primary_source.title: Order-Moment Transport and Hankel Determinants in Special-Function Inequalities

proof_boundary: This artifact rejects direct use of one positive order-moment transport criterion for the first Newman summand. It does not reject signed total positivity, prove the stable-gap curvature ceiling, order-four entry, PF-infinity, RH, or Lambda<=0.

rows.claim: The order-moment transport theorem turns Gamma-normalized completely-monotone averages into positive Hankel moment kernels.
The lambda=-100 first Newman summand has exactly the Gamma-average form tested by the theorem.
Application of the positive transport criterion requires complete monotonicity of the transformed kernel.
rows.proof_boundary: Necessary hypothesis for this theorem route, not a claim about all routes.

rows.claim: The transformed first-summand kernel increases at the origin and therefore fails complete monotonicity.
rows.proof_boundary: Exact formula with Arb sign certification.

rows.claim: If the criterion applied, it would produce positive Hankel moment orientation, not the reciprocal-Gamma signed orientation required here.
rows.formula: The transported A_t^(1) is a positive Mellin moment family and has nonnegative Hankel minors (ordinary log-convex orientation).

rows.claim: The positive order-moment transport theorem cannot be cited as the missing signed-Hankel bridge for the Xi first summand.
rows.formula: positive transport theorem != reciprocal sign-regular transport theorem
rows.proof_boundary: Rejects this theorem application only, not total-positivity methods in general.

rows.claim: Seek a signed reciprocal-Gamma analogue or continue the direct stable-gap curvature proof.
rows.formula: A signed reciprocal-Gamma transport theorem or the direct stable-gap curvature certificate is still required.

status: primary-source theorem-fit audit with exact first-summand complete-monotonicity obstruction
